candidate_llm_application: MAJLIS-GATE: dual-axis attention mask (causal-position AND status-priority) with per-token classifier-derived status embedding and tight status-inequality boundary

results.title: Prompt Injection as Role Confusion (2026/03)
results.reason: Direct functional twin — addresses representational role-hijack and role-boundary enforcement, which is exactly the status-axis violation MAJLIS-GATE prevents. kw>=2, sem>=0.7, func>=0.7 — triple hit.

results.title: Defending Against Prompt Injection With Few DefensiveTokens (ACM AISec)
results.reason: Defensive tokens + role-isolation overlap with per-token status embedding and status-axis mask. Triple hit.

results.title: Defending Prompt Injection DefensiveTokens (2025/07)
results.reason: Defensive tokens prevent injection via attention isolation — sem>=0.7 and func>=0.7.

results.title: PromptGuard Structured Injection Resilient LMs (Nature 2025)
results.reason: 4-layer structured defense covers gating/formatting axis. Triple hit.

results.title: The Instruction Hierarchy (OpenAI Wallace 2024)
results.reason: Direct precursor — 5-level privilege hierarchy via special role tokens IS the status-axis. MAJLIS-GATE only adds per-token mask. Triple hit.

results.title: Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy LLM Agents (2026/04)
results.reason: Tier-graded hierarchy covers the status-axis directly. Triple hit.

results.title: EvoSparse Token-Importance Sparse Attention (ACL 2026)
results.reason: Per-token importance-driven sparse attention mask — same mechanism family as per-token status mask. Triple hit at threshold.

results.title: 2026 State of LLM Security Bright Security
results.reason: Untrusted-context + least-privilege design principle is exactly the conceptual frame MAJLIS-GATE instantiates. Triple hit.

verdict_reason: Total hits = 8 (>=1). Candidate MAJLIS-GATE is fully covered by prior art: Instruction Hierarchy (2404.13208) and Many-Tier Hierarchy (2604.09443) cover status-priority privilege; EvoSparse (ACL26) covers per-token attention mask; DefensiveTokens (ACM AISec / 2507.07974) covers role-isolation via token-level mechanism. Recombination is incremental and falls within established effect clusters. Author's own novelty claim acknowledges recombination of existing components.
